MV PORTAITISSA
CYPRUS FLAG, 2006, LR
IMO No: 9323883
SINGLE DECK BULKCARRIER GEARLESS, IHI MARINE UNITED INC, YOKOHAMA SHIPYARD, JAPAN
DIESEL UNITED-SULZER 6RTA58T
DWAT 87,144 MT on 14.135 MTRS SSW
Grain Holds 98,961 CBM – 3,494,793 CFT
About 11.5 kts on about 27.5 mt VLSFO + 0.1 mt LSMGO laden up to summer draft / about 12.5 kts on about 27.5 mt VLSFO + 0.1
mt LSMGO in light ballast, basis up to max Beaufort Force 4 and/or Douglas Sea State 3 of maximum swell/waves height 1.25metres and basis no adverse currents, with favourable currents (if any) to be excluded from a voyage evaluation.
Vessel is allowed every 2/3 days to increase rpm at Master’s discretion for about 2/3 hours with additional consumption for Charterers account.
The speed and consumption so described is on average basis taken over the entire charter period, including ballast voyage(s), provided no deck cargo on board and provided no underwater parts fouling due to vessel remaining at or shifting (for any purpose) within a place, anchorage(s) and/or berth(s) or combined for an aggregated period of 15 or more days. No shifting and/or sailing within or outside the geographical limits of a port or from one port to another for less than continuous 48 hours should be treated as breaking the continuous running of 15 days. In such a case, it is a condition precedent to redelivery that Charterers to remain responsible for inspecting/cleaning (at Charterers’ time, risk and expense) the vessel\'s under water parts and super polishing the propeller prior to redelivery. Cleaning to be to the Owners’ satisfaction while the cleaning company as well as the equipment to be used to be approved by Owners. Performance is on the basis of 24 consecutive hours available good weather.
Vsl burns LSMGO whilst manoeuvring, also whilst in restricted or narrow or confined waters or in waters with restricted visibility.
The speed and consumption parameters provided in this description are not subject to review by charterers who are expressly prohibited from instructing Master to proceed at a speed and/or with a consumption other than as provided herein.
All methods of assessing the vessel’s performance during the charter, other than the good weather method, are excluded.
Port consumption idle about 3.7 MT VLSFO + 0.2 mt LSMGO per day.
Port consumption in cold or very warm weather add 1 MT VLSFO per day extra.
The above consumptions at Sea and in Port excludes VLSFO or LSMGO consumed for ballasting/deballasting and exchange of ballast, hold cleaning and washing,
closing/opening of hatches and bilge pumping.
Charterers to maintain sufficient quantity of LSMGO on board for such purposes.
All fuels supplied shall be homogenous and of quality suitable for burning in the vessel\'s engines
and auxiliaries and to conform to ISO 8217:2010(E) specifications (and any amendments thereto) of
quality RMG380 for IFO 380cst (VLSFO) with max sulphur content of 0.5% and which to be compliant with 2020 "sulphur content requirements" i.e. any sulphur content and related requirements as stipulated in marpol annex vi (as amended from time to time) and/or by any other applicable lawful authority and DMA for LSMGO. No mixing of fuels is allowed. ULO (Used Lubrication Oil) or other waste products are strictly not permitted and not to be included in the fuels supplied. LSMGO supplied to have max SULPHUR CONTENT OF 0.1%.
Charterers warrant that they will not bunker the vessel with bunkers by Iranian or Russian suppliers or with fuels/bunkers of Iranian or Russian origin. Bunkering by Iranian or Russian suppliers or bunkering in Iran or Russia or bunkering with fuels of Iranian or Russian origin is strictly prohibited.
